title: Three Civilians Found Tortured and Executed in Pale Township, Sagaing — Abducted as Human Shields
detail: Three civilian men from Chaungkan Village in Pale Township, Sagaing Region were found murdered on April 12, 2026 after being abducted by SAC forces during junta military operations and used as human shields. The victims were identified as Ko Wai Yan Phyo (21), Ko Kyaw Thiha (32), and Ko Phoe Tote (also known as Thant Zin Win, 38). Evidence confirmed they were subjected to torture, killed by gunshot wounds, and had been decapitated — consistent with documented SAC atrocity patterns in Sagaing. The use of civilians as human shields, followed by execution, represents a war crime under international humanitarian law. The discovery comes after weeks of SAC military operations in Sagaing in which at least 100 civilians were confirmed killed since the junta parliament convened on March 16. Human shields tactics have been documented across multiple townships in Sagaing and Mandalay regions, where the SAC forces civilians to walk ahead of military columns or into buildings to deter resistance attacks. Access restrictions by the SAC prevent immediate forensic verification by independent investigators, but local CDM networks and NLD-aligned community reporters documented the case.

media.caption: Three civilians found tortured and executed in Pale Township, Sagaing after being abducted as human shields

title: AA Commander Declares 2027 Deadline for Decisive Battles — Sittwe Siege Tightens Ahead of Thingyan Festival
detail: Arakan Army Commander-in-Chief Major General Twan Mrat Naing (also known as Aung Myo Kyaw) issued a strategic directive ordering AA troops to 'fight with the conviction that the remaining decisive battles must be won and completed before the end of this year and before the end of 2027,' signaling an intensified campaign to complete the conquest of Rakhine State and potentially expand into adjacent regions. As of April 12, AA fighters maintained positions within approximately 2 km of Sittwe city center, with the Shwe Min Gan Naval Base — struck on March 23 killing an estimated 30–40 junta soldiers — remaining contested. The SAC is defending Sittwe with approximately 2,000 infantry and 1,000 naval troops supported by 10 warships. In preparation for the Thingyan Water Festival (beginning April 13), the AA issued 6 public conduct guidelines prohibiting large gatherings in AA-controlled territory during the festival period — reflecting its role as the de facto administrative authority across most of Rakhine State. The AA's strategic 2027 deadline declaration represents a significant escalation in stated ambition, suggesting the organization intends to complete its campaign before a potential diplomatic settlement can consolidate the junta's cosmetic civilian transition.

media.caption: AA commander sets 2027 deadline for decisive battles as Sittwe siege intensifies
